risk._comment_risk_model: NO daily halt and NO hard position-count cap (user decision 2026-06-21). Instead: (1) per-trade risk SHRINKS as the day's realised loss grows, via daily_derisk_ladder [loss_pct_threshold, size_multiplier] — a bad day reduces size, never blocks a trade; (2) new swing entries are gated by an aggregate OPEN RISK budget (max_open_risk_pct=6%), not a fixed count, so good setups are taken until total risk is spent; (3) max_positions_ceiling=20 is only a sanity backstop against a runaway day. The ONLY thing that halts trading is the account-level catastrophe drawdown.
risk._comment_drawdown: PAPER catastrophe halt = 25%. For a FUNDED prop account, set max_total_drawdown_pct = max_total_drawdown_pct_funded (8.0) AND set trend.base_exposure = 0.33. This account-level stop is the ONLY hard halt (protects a funded account from prop-firm termination); the daily de-risk ladder protects capital well before it trips.

trend._comment: TREND SLEEVE — 30% of capital (capital_allocation). Faber GTAA monthly ETF rebalance. exposure 1.0 = fully invest the 30% sleeve. memory/adaptive.py walks exposure down the ladder for capital preservation on drawdown and restores after recovery. Every change is logged + sent to Telegram. Disjoint universe from the swing sleeve (ETFs only) so the two strategies never trade the same symbol.

swing._comment: SWING SLEEVE — 70% of capital. Daily D1-trend / H4-pullback system (strategy/scorer.py). Risk-based sizing at risk_per_trade_pct of full equity per trade, gated by the open-risk budget. Trades a disjoint stock watchlist (config.SWING_WATCHLIST) so it never collides with the trend ETF sleeve.
